subclass.name: Path of the Broken Oath
subclassFeatures: Path of the Broken Oath|Barbarian|PHB|Broken Oath|SMRBarbarians|3

subclass.name: Path of the Coastal Wizard
subclassFeatures: Path of the Coastal Wizard|Barbarian|PHB|Coastal Wizard|SMRBarbarians|3

subclass.name: Path of the Misty Stepper
subclassFeatures: Path of the Misty Stepper|Barbarian|PHB|Misty Stepper|SMRBarbarians|3

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Bloodline
entries: The Path of the Bloodline is rarely chosen — most barbarians who walk this path were always destined to. Magic pulses in their blood, due to a mighty ancestor or some other quirk of heritage. In a different individual, this might manifest as sorcerous powers — but a Bloodline barbarian instead learns to access it through their primal rage.

Bloodline barbarians may come from any background, though something about their lineage is often worthy of note. Similarly, they may come from any alignment — their ancestors' character has no bearing on how they choose to use their power.

entries.name: Path of the Bloodline Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: Your eyes glow with sorcerous fire when you {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.
Your were born with mystical tattoos, which glow when you cast spells.
According to family stories, you look exactly like one particular ancestor.

You have a bad habit of attempting to cast a spell while not {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}. This fails, makes you completely furious, and then you {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: Sorcerous Bloodline
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, your magical inheritance begins to express itself. Select {@filter one bloodline from the {@homebrew included options|list at the end of this document}|optionalfeatures|source=|feature type=SMRBarb:SB|miscellaneous=}.

Additionally, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill, and you learn the spell {@spell detect magic}, with Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You can cast it a number of times per day equal to your proficiency modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Sorcerous Origins
entries: As an alternative to the {@homebrew included options|list below}, select the origin spell list from any {@class sorcerer} subclass which has one, and work with your DM to choose an appropriate damage type.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Rage
entries: Also at 3rd level, your rage enables you to unleash your bloodline's power. As you gain levels, you learn the spells associated with your bloodline, as shown on the bloodline's spells table. You can only cast them while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, and casting any of your bloodline spells counts as dealing damage for purposes of maintaining your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|}. You can maintain {@condition concentration} on your bloodline spells while {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}.
You can cast one spell of each spell level (1st-5th), regaining all casts after completing a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your bloodline spells. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a bloodline spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

subclassFeature.name: Shield of Sorcery
entries: Also at 3rd level, while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can add your Charisma modifier to your armor class in place of your Dexterity or Constitution modifier (your choice) when you use your {@classFeature Unarmored Defense|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: Inherited Defenses
entries: At 6th level, your bloodline gives you tremendous resilience. When you begin {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + half your barbarian level. Additionally, while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you gain resistance to the damage type specified by your Sorcerous Bloodline.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Assault
entries: At 10th level, you gain greater mastery of your bloodline's power. When you cast a bloodline spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.
Additionally, when you cast your bloodline spells, you can add half your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) to your spell save DC and spell attack modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodline Transformation
entries: At 14th level, your inborn power finds full expression in your rage. While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you gain the following additional benefits:

items: Your weapon attacks deal {@damage 1d6} bonus damage of the type specified by your Sorcerous Bloodline.
When you cast one of your bloodline spells, you gain a 60-foot flying speed until the end of your turn.
When you target an enemy with one of your bloodline spells, you gain advantage on attacks against that enemy until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Broken Oath
entries: Not all who aspire to the {@class paladin|PHB|paladin's} path are able to walk it. Those who stumble may fall onto the Path of the Broken Oath. Stripped of any divine or oath-borne powers, these warriors fall into fearsome rages, unleashing a fury upon their enemies that may have its roots in inner shame. Some may seek redemption, aiming to regain their paladin status; others may embrace failure, reveling in their barbaric aggression.

An oathbreaking barbarian may come from any circumstance, but the key to their story is how they choose to handle their fallen status. Most began with virtuous codes; an antipaladin is less likely to be bothered by a broken oath, using it simply as fuel for more villainous deeds. But once a paladin falls, they may choose whether to climb back upward, or continue on their barbaric path.

entries.name: Path of the Broken Oath Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: Dark clouds swirl in your eyes when you {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.
You still insist on wearing your paladin raiments, despite rips, tears, and stains.
You keep a holy symbol, which is always painfully hot to the touch.
You have tear streaks tattooed on your face.
Sometimes when you hit things exceptionally hard, you yell "Smite!" as if it would accomplish anything.

entries.name: Anatomy of a Fall
entries: If a paladin falls (or a fallen paladin is redeemed) as part of your game's story, there are two broad approaches to take with this subclass. You can either replace their {@class paladin} levels with barbarian levels, or multiclass them together. This subclass aims to work well for either circumstance; DMs and players should work together to decide what will be most narratively satisfying in their game.

subclassFeature.name: Oathbreaker
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, your rage becomes fueled by your shame, your grief, and your failure. Choose whether to walk the path of Redemption or Embrace, which determines certain aspects of your subclass features. You can change this choice whenever you gain a level in this class or in the {@class paladin} class.

subclassFeature.name: Fallen Rage
entries: At 3rd level, your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} takes on different properties based on your choice of path.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: You seek to reclaim your dignity and self. When you enter your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} and at the start of each of your turns while you're {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: You lash out savagely in your misery. While you're {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, all your weapon attacks deal bonus damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
Regardless of your choice, you can use your Charisma modifier instead of Dexterity for your {@classFeature Unarmored Defense|Barbarian|PHB|1} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Broken Aura
entries: At 6th level, you learn to harness some measure of your former holy might.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: While you are raging, you exude a faint aura of divine wrath. As a reaction when a creature within 15 feet of you takes damage, you can cause it to take extra damage equal to twice your combined barbarian and {@class paladin} levels. This damage is either necrotic or radiant, which you choose when you gain this feature.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: Your savage presence is sufficient to intimidate your foes. As a bonus action or as part of entering your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}, you can terrorize all creatures of your choice within 15 feet. Each creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be {@condition frightened} until the end of your next turn.
You can activate your broken aura a number of times equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier, regaining 1 use after finishing a short rest and all uses after finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Severed Oath
entries: At 10th level, you can feel the faintest glimmers of your old Oath, and either call upon it or spurn it to fuel your abilities.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: You learn the spells {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell lesser restoration}, and {@spell revivify}.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: You learn the spells {@spell hellish rebuke}, {@spell spectral champion|SpellsThatDontSuck}, and {@spell unbridled fury|SpellsThatDontSuck}.

You can cast each spell you learn from this feature once without expending a spell slot. Once you've cast a spell in this way, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells. You can still cast these spells or maintain {@condition concentration} on them, even if you're {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}.

subclassFeature.name: Shattered Faith
entries: At 14th level, your broken path has carried you so far it feels it must be nearing its end.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: You must pay more penance before you are redeemed. Creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you can add your Charisma modifier to their saving throws. If any creature does so, the next time you make a saving throw before the end of your next turn, you roll with disadvantage.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: You will never be redeemed, and you now revel in slaughter. Anytime a creature of challenge rating 1/4 or higher dies within 15 feet of you, you gain temporary hit points equal to half your combined barbarian and {@class paladin} levels.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Coastal Wizard
entries: The Path of the Coastal Wizard is not for everyone. It is not, ultimately, for most people. It requires years of diligent study and meditation, decades of professional experience, and a commitment to detail that can only be described as "lackadaisical". The Coastal Wizard is a barbarian who rages to forget, insistent that the thing they said this morning is absolutely true until they say another thing that contradicts it tomorrow. Their furious tantrums leave only splintered furniture and punctured balloons in their wake.

Coastal Wizards are universally chaotic - the very heart of the philosophy demands a belief in making rules up on the spot, without any real consideration for the rules one has made before. Good and evil are rarely relevant concepts to a Coastal Wizard, though, as their thought process cannot be enclosed in such rigid boxes.

entries.name: Requirements: Elf
items: Also, you have to be an elf. That's just how it is. There are plenty of elves, just pick the strongest kind or whatever.

entries.name: Path of the Coastal Wizard Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: You cannot stop tweeting for some reason.
Your friends all suspect a shadowy toymaker is secretly dictating your actions.
When nobody is looking, you love to roll in piles of money.
You begin {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|Raging} anytime someone points out one of your contradictions.
You frequently give away your toys, to encourage others to play with them.
Whenever your toys get too popular, you get mad and take them away.

subclassFeature.name: Best At Games
entries: When you choose this path at 3rd level, you immediately become an unimpeachable expert at game design. You gain proficiency with all {@item gaming set|PHB|gaming sets} ({@item dice set|PHB|dice}, {@item dragonchess set|PHB|dragonchess}, {@item playing card set|PHB|playing cards}, and {@item three-dragon ante set|PHB|three-dragon ante}). When you make ability checks using a {@item gaming set|PHB}, you can choose to use your Strength modifier in place of the ability you would normally use. After you do so, the {@item gaming set|PHB} is destroyed.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Wizarding
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain a special ability modifier, called your Wizarding modifier. You can calculate it by taking the negative of the sum of your Intelligence and Wisdom ability modifiers.

entries.name: Wizarding save DC
entries.text: 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wizarding modifier

entries: When you enter your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}, you go into a state of serene contemplation and carefully-considered strategy. While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, every melee attack deals bonus psychic damage equal to your Wizarding Modifier.

entries.name: Practical Tactical Brilliance
entries: For example, a Coastal Wizard with Intelligence 8 (-1) and Wisdom 6 (-2) has a Wizarding modifier of +3, and so gets a damage bonus of +3 from this feature.

subclassFeature.name: I Made It Better
entries: At 6th level, your keen mind makes you a master at evaluating anything and figuring out how to improve it. You gain proficiency in {@quickref improvised weapons|PHB|1|0}. While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can use any object around you as an {@quickref improvised weapons|PHB|1|0|improvised weapon} which deals {@damage 1d12} bludgeoning damage.

When you hit with an {@quickref improvised weapons|PHB|1|0|improvised weapon}, you can choose to make it better as part of the attack. If you hit, the attack deals an additional {@damage 1d12} damage. Afterwards, the object is no longer suitable as a weapon, and any checks made to use it for its intended purpose are made at disadvantage.
You can make things better a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Backwards Compatible
entries: At 10th level, you gain perfect knowledge of everything behind you. You gain proficiency in the {@skill History} skill, or expertise if you are already proficient.

As a bonus action, anytime a creature is behind you, you can declare them backwards compatible. Anytime you take damage from a backwards compatible creature, you can reduce that damage by your {@subclassFeature Tactical Wizarding|Barbarian|PHB|Coastal Wizard|SMRBarbarians|3|SMRBarbarians|Wizarding modifier}. Only one creature can be backwards compatible at a time.
As 5th Edition has no official facing rules, there are no rules for what it means for a creature to be behind you. Make some up. You're good at game design!

subclassFeature.name: Ultimate Wizard
entries: Starting at 14th level, you are beginning to feel the keen edge of the martial/caster disparity, and you do not like it, so you will fix it. As a reaction to a spell being cast within 60 feet of you, you can learn the spell.

You can cast the spell one time, using your {@subclassFeature Tactical Wizarding|Barbarian|PHB|Coastal Wizard|SMRBarbarians|3|SMRBarbarians|Wizarding modifier}. It follows all the normal rules for spellcasting, including components, though you can substitute a wizard hat you are wearing for any material components that do not have a specified cost. You can cast this spell even while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, and casting this spell in this way counts as attacking for the purpose of maintaining your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

You can learn a number of spells in this way equal to your proficiency bonus, though you can only know one at a time. After a long rest, you forget any spells you have learned in this way, and regain all uses of this ability.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Forgewrought
entries: Some barbarians choose their path — others are built for it. The Path of the Forgewrought is for warriors who've undergone dreadful modifications, replacing flesh with fire and steel to become a mighty war machine. Their augmentations may be mild or sweeping, and voluntary or coerced, but they are always defining.

Forgewrought barbarians hold no particular allegiance, just as the stories of their creation can be widely varied. Nobles very rarely find themselves subjected to such modifications, though, as power and wealth go far to protect a warrior from the augmentation table.

entries.name: Path of the Forgewrought Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: You howl with pain when you activate your augmentations.
Your body is studded with hoses and pneumatic tubes.
Sometimes when you provide {@subclassFeature Forgecraft Assistance|Barbarian|PHB|Forgewrought|SMRBarbarians|10}, you accidentally call it Forgewrought Assimilation.
Your blood is black, with a sludgy texture.
Your voice alternates between yells of fury and a robotic monotone.
After using your augmentations, your skin is fiery-hot to the touch.

subclassFeature.name: Routine Maintenance
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, you gain the knowledge and skills to maintain your augmentations. You gain proficiency with {@item smith's tools|PHB}, or another tool of your choice if you're already proficient.

subclassFeature.name: Augmented Rage
entries: Also at 3rd level, you undergo major modifications to your body. Select two modifications at 3rd level and another at 6th level. All your modifications have a base effect and an additional effect while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}. The first time you take a long rest after leveling up in this class, you can remove one augmentation you have and replace it with another.

entries.name: Iron Fist
items: You modify your arms, replacing them with metallic fists of tremendous power. Your iron fists count as simple melee weapons with the light property, with which you can make unarmed strikes, and deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier on a hit.

items.name: Raging Fist
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, when you hit an enemy with your iron fist, you can make one additional attack with it as a bonus action.

items.name: Magic Hands
entries: A character might choose to build around Iron Fists for their primary weapon, and run into some trouble once they start needing magical weapons. This is a common pitfall for archetypes that add natural weapons. In a pinch, you can use Magma Core to make your fists deal fire damage. Longer-term, I'd suggest DMs offer an Iron Fists character appropriate magical items to address this.

entries.name: Integrated Weapon
items: You build a weapon into your body, incorporating it into one of your limbs directly. This weapon must not have the two-handed or heavy properties, and you must be proficient with it. You can't throw the weapon or be disarmed of it, and you have advantage on any ability checks to conceal it. It still requires a hand to wield as normal. You can draw or stow it as a bonus action, or as part of entering your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

items.name: Spring-Loaded Attack
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, your integrated weapon deals an additional die of damage on the first attack after you draw it.

entries.name: Armor Plating
items: You attach additional armor plating directly to your body. If you're not wearing armor, this gives you a +1 bonus to your AC; if you're wearing armor, its AC is increased by 1. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC. If you have access to {@item smith's tools|PHB}, you can change your suit of integrated armor with 1 hour's work as part of a long rest.

items.name: Armored Assault
entries: When you enter your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}, you gain temporary hit points equal to 1 + your Barbarian level.

items.name: That Sounds Uncomfortable?
entries: If your table applies penalties to characters who sleep in armor, don't do so for a Forgewrought with Armor Plating. They're built for this, after all.

entries.name: Magma Core
items: You embed a volcanic power source directly within your body, fueling yourself with magma. You gain resistance to fire damage, and when you make a weapon attack, you can choose for the attack to deal fire damage instead of its normal damage type.

items.name: Volcanic Spray
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can cause an eruption of lava to fly off your weapon. Once on each of your turns when you hit an enemy with a weapon attack, you can choose to deal an additional {@damage 1d6} fire damage to the target and to another creature within 5 feet of it.

entries.name: Voltaic Jet
items: You attach powerful spark generators to your legs. Your movement speed increases by 10 feet, and your jumping distance is doubled.

items.name: Voltaic Leap
entries: When you enter your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}, you can activate your jet to take off in a blast of electricity. Every creature within 5 feet of you takes {@damage 1d4} lightning damage. If you then jump at least 10 feet, every creature within 5 feet of where you land also takes {@damage 1d4} lightning damage.

entries.name: Launcher Rods
items: You attach pneumatic rods to your arms, giving yourself tremendously powerful throwing ability. Your normal range for all thrown weapons is doubled.

items.name: Power Throw
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can overexert your launcher rods to hurl a thrown weapon at tremendous force. When you make a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, you can choose to increase its normal range to be equal to its long range. If the attack hits, it deals {@damage 2d6} additional damage of the weapon's type, and the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be knocked {@condition prone}. You can do this once, regaining the ability the next time you {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: Emergency Maintenance
entries: At 6th level, you modify your body for extraordinary resilience. When you suffer damage that would drop you to 0 hit points, you can make a Strength ({@item smith's tools|PHB}) check with a DC equal to 10 or half the damage dealt, whichever is higher. On a success, you drop to 1 hit point instead. Once you attempt to reduce damage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Forgecraft Assistance
entries: At 10th level, you learn to apply your own modifications to others. Over a long rest, you can construct a portable version of any of your current augmentations, which you can attach to a willing creature as an action. The augmentation functions for 1 hour, after which it becomes inoperative. Before you can construct another, you must remove the first as an action, dealing {@damage 3d8} necrotic damage to the creature in the process.
The recipient only gains the augmentation's basic effect, not any added effects triggered during {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: Enhanced Augmentations
entries: At 14th level, you can tailor your augmentations, enhancing their power. Any augmentations you have receive an additional bonus.

items.name: Iron Fist
entries: Your Iron Fist's damage increases to {@damage 2d4}.

items.name: Integrated Weapon
entries: Your Spring-Loaded Attack's bonus damage increases to two additional dice.

items.name: Armor Plating
entries: Your Armor Plating's AC bonus increases to 2.

items.name: Magma Core
entries: Your Volcanic Spray's damage increases to {@damage 2d6}.

items.name: Voltaic Jet
entries: Your Voltaic Jet's movement speed bonus increases to 20 feet, and your Voltaic Leap's damage increases to {@damage 2d4}.

items.name: Launcher Rods
entries: Your Power Throw's bonus damage increases to {@damage 4d6}.
Portable augmentations created through your {@subclassFeature Forgecraft Assistance|Barbarian|PHB|Forgewrought|SMRBarbarians|10} don't get this bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Misty Stepper
entries: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Misty Stepper just really f'n love {@spell misty step}. What's it to you?

entries.name: Path of the Misty Stepper Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: You're pretty sure you were some sort of elf in a past life.
You prefer to sleep outside on foggy nights.
If someone invented some sort of fog machine, you would absolutely carry one with you everywhere.
You have nightmares about not being able to teleport.
Your runner-up path was {@subclass Coastal Wizard|Barbarian|PHB|SMRBarbarians|Coastal Wizardry}.

subclassFeature.name: Mystic Steps
entries: Anytime you cast {@spell misty step} through one of your subclass features, your spellcasting modifier for it is Constitution.

subclassFeature.name: Misty Rage
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB} gives you the ability to move unconstrained around the battlefield. When you activate your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB} and as a bonus action on any of your subsequent turns while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can cast {@spell misty step}. If you hit a creature with an attack for the first time immediately after teleporting next to it, your attack deals {@damage 1d10} bonus force damage.

You can cast {@spell misty step} in this way a number of times equal to your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB} uses plus your Constitution modifier. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Emergency Teleport
entries: At 6th level, you can fall back on your favorite spell instinctively when threatened. As a reaction before you would drop to 0 hit points, you can cast {@spell misty step}. If you do so, roll a d10. On a roll of 1-5, you dodge the triggering damage entirely and reduce it to 0.

subclassFeature.name: Misty Camaraderie
entries: At 10th level, your marvelous teleportation extends to your allies. When you could cast {@spell misty step} through your {@subclassFeature Misty Rage|Barbarian|PHB|Misty Stepper|SMRBarbarians|3} feature, you can instead empower an ally to do the same. It still consumes a use of your feature, and is cast using your spellcasting modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior of the Mist
entries: At 14th level, your penchant for short-distance teleportation reaches its peak. The bonus damage of your {@subclassFeature Misty Rage|Barbarian|PHB|Misty Stepper|SMRBarbarians|3} feature increases to {@damage 2d10}, and anytime you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you regain one use of the feature.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Risen
entries: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Risen are are those who died, and then, somehow, didn't; their power comes from this anchor to the grave. At some point, somehow, they touched the eternal boundary between life and death, and instead of crossing, came back changed. They are rarely able to explain why, but they can reach back across that boundary to harness mysterious powers

Path of the Risen barbarians come from all walks of life — the thing that unifies them is a lethal history. They often did not choose their path, but now find themselves with unnatural might and must make use of it. Their alignments may also vary, as some find their deathly powers to be a corrupting temptation, while others came away from their near-death experience determined to lead a purer life.

entries.name: Path of the Risen Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: You saw exactly how and when you will die. You won't tell anybody.
You've never been sure if your soul's really the one that came back.
When you {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}, your eyes turn coal-black.
Every night when you sleep, your allies hear you reliving your death in your dreams.
You know exactly how your companions will die.
Anyone who leans in close to you detects the scent of grave dirt.

subclassFeature.name: Strength of the Grave
entries: At 3rd level, your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} allows you to tap into an unhallowed energy from the other side. While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can choose for your attacks to deal necrotic damage instead of their normal damage type.

Additionally, if a creature of CR 1/4 or greater dies within 15 feet while you are raging, you can use your reaction to tear its spirit asunder and blast its energy outward. One creature of your choice within 15 feet of the target must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier, taking necrotic damage equal to {@damage 1d8 + #$prompt_number:min=3,max=20,title=Enter your barbarian level,default=3$#|1d8 + your barbarian level|Strength of the Grave} on a failure or half as much on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Back from the Dead
entries: At 6th level, your stubborn hold on life grows to a frightening strength. You gain the following abilities:

items: You have resistance to necrotic damage.
When you roll a death saving throw, you can treat a roll of 19 as if you rolled a 20 on the die.
When you fail a death saving throw, you can choose to instead treat the result as if it were a 20. You can do so once, regaining the ability after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Cross the Boundary
entries: At 10th level, you can summon the spirits of the slain back to obey your commands. You learn the {@spell unseen servant} and {@spell speak with dead} spells. You can cast each one once without expending a spell slot, regaining the ability to do so after a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Spiritual Communion
entries: Also at 10th level, you can summon a spirit to impart its skills directly into your mind. As an action, you can choose to gain proficiency in one skill with which you are not proficient, or you can double your proficiency bonus for one skill with which you are proficient. This effect fades after 1 hour.
You can use this ability once, regaining its use after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Swarm of Spirits
entries: At 14th level, the spirits you call back from the other side are ravenous and tear into your foes. As a bonus action, or automatically after you roll a 19 or 20 on a death saving throw, you can summon a swarm of spirits, which swirl around you in a 15-foot radius and steal the life from your foes. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet suffers {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage at the start of each of your turns, and you regain 2 hit points for each creature affected. The spirits last for 1 minute, until you fall unconscious, or until you dismiss them as a bonus action.
You can use summon spirits as a bonus action once, regaining the ability to do so after a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Tidebringer
entries: The Path of the Tidebringer is for barbarians who wish to call upon the sea's fury. Perhaps they swore an oath to a vengeful god of the deeps, or were empowered during a watery brush with death. Some may seek to defend the ocean's glory, while others wish to see the waves rise up and wash away their enemies.

Tidebringers may come from any background, though almost all will have some connection to the sea. Their alignments vary widely, as well - noble and lawful defenders of the waves walk this path alongside destroyers who would see landborne life washed away.

entries.name: Path of the Tidebringer Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: You refuse to eat ocean-dwelling creatures, claiming it "wouldn't feel right."
You begin each day with a morning swim when possible.
You immediately {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} anytime you see someone throw trash into the sea.
Your hair contains tangles of seaweed, and you have a constant scent of saltwater.
When you rage, your body takes on a silvery sheen like fish scales.
You end each day with an evening swim when possible.

subclassFeature.name: Ocean Dweller
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in water vehicles, or another tool of your choice if you are already proficient. Additionally, you gain the ability to hold your breath for 1 hour, and a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. If you already have a swimming speed, your swimming speed increases by 15 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Sea's Fury
entries: Also at 3rd level, your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} enables you to channel the sea's might. When you begin {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can choose one of the following benefits, which lasts for the duration of your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

entries.name: Depths
entries: Whenever you deal damage to a target with a melee weapon attack, they take an additional amount of cold damage equal to your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} damage bonus.

entries.name: Waves
entries: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack, you can force them to make a Strength saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. On a failure, they are knocked {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Emissary of the Waves
entries: At 6th level, you are able to summon greater reserves of oceanic power. You gain the ability to breathe water, and choose an additional benefit which you can change after any short or long rest.

entries.name: Depths
entries: You gain resistance to fire and cold damage.

entries.name: Waves
entries: As a reaction when an enemy misses you with a melee attack, you can move up to half your speed without provoking attacks of opportunity.

subclassFeature.name: Wavecaller
entries: At 10th level, you can summon the fury of the waves directly. You learn the {@spell crashing wave|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell dancing wave|KibblesTasty:CC}, and {@spell waterspout|KibblesTasty:CC} spells, and can cast them with Strength as your spellcasting ability modifier. You can cast these spells and {@condition concentration||concentrate} on them while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, and dealing damage with them counts as dealing damage for purposes of maintaining your {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.
You can cast each of these spells as an action or a bonus action one time without expending a spell slot, and are able to do so again after completing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ocean's Wrath
entries: At 14th level, your command over the ocean's power grows greater. When you begin {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1|PHB|raging}, you can choose to fully embody the wrath of the ocean. If you do so, you gain both benefits of your {@subclassFeature Sea's Fury|Barbarian|PHB|Tidebringer|SMRBarbarians|3} ability, and both effects apply to creatures of your choice within 5 feet of the creature you attack.
You can {@classFeature rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} in this way once, regaining the ability to do so after a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Aberrant Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is psychic.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

rows: {@spell arms of Hadar}, {@spell dissonant whispers}, {@spell mind sliver|TCE}

entries: {@note This bloodline is not included in the base document. It has been included here for completeness, based on the {@subclass Aberrant Mind|Sorcerer|PHB|TCE} subclass. Additionally, the author has stated that cantrips can be included at the DM's discretion, so the {@spell mind sliver|TCE} spell is included for completeness. Also, the damage type for this bloodline is listed here as psychic because the . However, the author has stated in multiple places that the damage type should be a choice made by the player and DM.}

optionalfeature.name: Clockwork Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is force.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

rows: {@spell alarm}, {@spell protection from evil and good}

entries: {@note This bloodline is not included in the base document. It has been included here for completeness, based on the {@subclass Clockwork Soul|Sorcerer|PHB|TCE} subclass. The damage type for this bloodline is listed here as force. However, the author has stated in multiple places that the damage type should be a choice made by the player and DM.}

optionalfeature.name: Divine Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is radiant.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

rows: {@spell bless}, {@spell protection from evil and good}
{@spell dispel evil and good}, {@spell holy fire|SpellsThatDontSuck}

optionalfeature.name: Draconic Bloodline
entries: For a draconic bloodline, select one color out of the chromatic and metallic dragon types. Your bloodline damage type is the associated damage type of that color dragon.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below. For spells that deal elemental damage, they instead deal the damage type associated with your dragon color.

optionalfeature.name: Shadowed Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is necrotic.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

optionalfeature.name: Storm Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is lightning.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

optionalfeature.name: Wild Magic Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is force.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.
